title: LRRK2 and LAMP1 immunofluorescence staining in various cell lines
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4hj1","text":"This protocol is being used to test the antibody   , as well as  . Please note that after multiple rounds of optimization, anti-LRRK2 antibody generates significant non-specific signals in the LRRK2 KO cell lines so this antibody may not be reliable for Immunofluorescence staining experiments. The protocol can be used for general IF experiments.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":49,"length":1},{"key":1,"offset":64,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"9bbk8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"id":960420,"guid":"437BE57F341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Recombinant Anti-LRRK2 antibody [MJFF2 (c41-2)] (ab133474)","mol_weight":0,"sku":"","url":"https://www.abcam.com/products/primary-antibodies/lrrk2-antibody-mjff2-c41-2-ab133474.html?productWallTab=ShowAll","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":22,"name":"Abcam","link":"http://www.abcam.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"id":960421,"guid":"437BE5D3341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Anti-LAMP1 antibody [1D4B] (ab25245)","mol_weight":0,"sku":"","url":"https://www.abcam.com/products/primary-antibodies/lamp1-antibody-1d4b-ab25245.html","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":22,"name":"Abcam","link":"http://www.abcam.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"eq9qp","text":"GDB buffer was used as blocking buffer in the optimized protocol.\nThis protocol uses ibidi 8 Well Chamber µ-Slides and will coat them with Fibronectin. Standard volume would be 300 uL for each well. Volumes will need altering for wells, other plates and slides. \nAll steps are performed at room temperature (RT) on the lab bench, except for methanol permeabilization.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"8bf8p","text":"Important note:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"89vvd","text":"While LAMP1 immunofluorescence staining robustly gives expected results, after multiple rounds of optimization, anti-LRRK2 antibody generates significant non-specific signals in the LRRK2 KO cell lines in our hand. This is a warning that this antibody may not be reliable for Immunofluorescence staining experiments. However, this protocol can be used widely for IF experiments.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ehctt","text":"Please check This google sheet to learn more about the cell lines and conditions tested for the antibody","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":13,"length":17}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"6A677850325C11EEBFC2D71324883B23","url":"https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1N9OoLE8lkQvIOl_ZKsW6p-rRGdG0PVM00Uzb1VqPhU4/edit?usp=sharing"}}}}

distilled H2O to make a volume of 1 L
The pH of the final solution will be 7.4. This buffer can be stored for up to 1 mo at 4°C","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dpd5r","text":"GDB buffer
30mM NaPi (sodium phosphate) pH 7.4
0.45mM NaCl
0.2% porcine (or fish) gelatin
In ddH2O","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dnhip","text":"Kim wipes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e6lrk","text":"Ethanol (100%, stored in dark chemicals cupboard)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aq19","text":"Water (double-deionised H2O from Milli-Q, “MQ-H2O”)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 300 µL of 11 ug/mL fibronectin into each ibidi well. Incubate at RT for 01:00:00 .
Rinse the wells with PBS for 3 times

Make GDB buffer if necessary Stock Amount needed C Final conc. E 1 (0.1M) NaPi pH 7.4 15 mL 30 mM 2 (5M) NaCl 0.0045 mL 0.45 mM 3 Gelatin 0.1 g 0.2 % 4 H2O 34.9955 mL 5 Total 50 mL � Recipe to make GDB buffer

Seed adherent cells to 40-80% confluency in each well. Incubate at 37 °C Overnight to get optimal seeding. For RAW264.7 cells, 6x10^4 in 300uL is a good starting point. Less would be needed for other typical cell lines since macrophage cells are smaller. It is suggested to start with two different cell concentrations for the first time. Please refer to this page for more information

Apply any drug treatments or controls and note time of additions before proceeding with fixing. As an example, Chloroquine diphosphate salt Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #C6628-25G and Leu-Leu methyl ester hydrobromide Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #L7393-500MG can be added at desired concentrations for 03:00:00 Drugs MW - g/mol mM weight in 1 mL E 1 LLOME 339.27 1000 339.27 mg/1mL 2 CQ 515.86 100 51.586 mg/1mL Drug stock recipe and concentrations In each well, add 1 in 1000 ( 1 millimolar (mM) for LLOME and 0.1 millimolar (mM) for CQ)
Put 100% ethanol on ice before proceeding with next steps.

Bring GDB buffer to Room temperature . Prepare and prewarm fixation buffer (4% sucrose, 3% PFA in 1xPBS) at 37 °C 3% PFA is preferred from 4% as it reduces autofluorescence Need 375 µL Pierce™ 16% Formaldehyde (w/v) Methanol-free Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #28906 and 0.08 g Sucrose Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #S0389 , dissove with 2 mL PBS.
Get cells, aspirate media and immediately add prewarmed fixation buffer. Incubate for 00:10:00 at 37 °C
Aspirate PFA, rinse 2x with PBS and wash two times with PBS for 5 minutes each, 00:10:00 in total

Quenching: Only necessary when staining the day of fixation. Incubate 3 times of 10 minutes ( 00:30:00 in total) using 0.4% Ammonium chloride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #254134 ( 75 millimolar (mM) )

Optional step: For half of the samples, choose to add another permeablisation step with 300 µL 100% ethanol at -20°C for 00:20:00 . Leave the rest at Room temperature in GDB buffer. This step helps to increase contrast when imaging Recombinant Anti-LRRK2 antibody [MJFF2 (c41-2)] (ab133474) Abcam , but doesn't help with the issue of non-specific signals when LRRK2 KO cell lines are used. ethanol/methanol permeablisation at -20 °C will disrupt the microtubule staining. Apply this step with caution when other antibodies are used.
Apsirate ethanol, wash 2x with PBS.
Prepare GDB + 0.05% Triton X-100 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #X100 freshly. Add 300 µL for 00:10:00 . 5 µL in 10 mL GDB buffer. Can be stored at 4 °C for a couple of days.
Aspirate Triton X-100, wash 2x with GDB.
Block cells with 300 µL GDB for 00:30:00 at Room temperature

During blocking, prepare final concentration of 1 ug/mL for both Recombinant Anti-LRRK2 antibody [MJFF2 (c41-2)] (ab133474) Abcam and Anti-LAMP1 antibody [1D4B] (ab25245) Abcam solution with GDB and 50000 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes - each ibidi dish well requires 150 µL 150 µL is the minimum required amount. 200 µL would be sufficient and optimal to cover the entire well

Take most of the supernatant and place in new tube. Mix to get even concentration (due to Gelatin Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #G2500 in there, there will be a small clear precipitate)

Aspirate blocking solution and incubate cells with 150 µL of primary antibody solution Overnight at 4 °C on a table-top shaker Primary antibody incubation can be as short as 2 hours without affecting the final outcome When incubating overnight, consider wrapping up the dish with parafilm and/or put the dish in a humidity chamber to prevent the well from drying up
Bring GDB to Room temperature . Aspirate antibody solution and rinse 2x with GDB.
Wash 3x 5min Room temperature RT with GDB, 00:15:00 in total

Prepare 1:500 Alexa-flour conjugated secondary antibody solution with GDB - each ibidi well requires 200 µL . When LRRK2 and LAMP1 are co-stained, Goat Anti-Rabbit IgG H&L (Alexa Fluor® 647) (ab150079) Abcam and Goat Anti-Rat IgG H&L (Alexa Fluor® 488) (ab150157) Abcam are used at final concentration of 4 ug/mL . Lower concentration did not help with the issue of non-specific LRRK2 signal.
Incubate cells with 200 µL of secondary antibody solution for 01:30:00 at Room temperature and protect from light with an ice box.
15 minutes before the incubation is finished, add DAPI Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #D1306 to a final concentration of 1 ug/mL and incubate until the last step finishes.
Rinse cells with 1xPBS for 5 times
Apply 2-4 drops of FluorSave™ Reagent Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #345789 hard mounting media in each well and swirl to make sure the bottom is fully covered.
Allow to air-dry for 00:10:00 at Room temperature .
Image within 48 h of mounting or the sample will begin to deteriorate (bright debris impeding imaging) and visibly autofluoresce in red.
creator: Siyu Chen
